steps.title: Use the visible point
steps.content: The graph shown is $y=f(x)+4$, not $y=f(x)$. The easiest visible point is the $y$-intercept: the graph passes through $(0,8)$. That means when $x=0$, the shifted output is $8$.

steps.title: Test the shifted choices in Desmos
steps.content: Since the displayed graph is $f(x)+4$, add $4$ to each answer choice and evaluate at $x=0$. A one-row Desmos table shows only choice B gives the visible output $8$.

steps.title: Confirm with the graph
steps.content: For choice B, $f(x)=-3^x+5$, so the displayed curve would be $y=-3^x+9$. At $x=0$, this gives $y=-1+9=8$, matching the graph. The curve also decreases sharply as $x$ increases, matching the shown shape.

steps.title: Eliminate the other constants
steps.content: Choices A, C, and D make the shifted $y$-intercept $4$, $11$, and $12$, respectively. The graph's shifted $y$-intercept is $8$, so only choice B works. Choice B is correct.
